questions.description: In 2007, four close college friends, David, Paul, Matthew and Alex, bought… Advise the parties.
questions.text: In 2007, four close college friends, David, Paul, Matthew and Alex, bought a duplex apartment together. Title to the apartment was not registered in the Land Registry. They each paid an equal share of the purchase price. Shortly afterwards, David lost his job and decided to emigrate and sold his interest to Paul. In 2006, Paul unexpectedly became and decided, without legal advice, that it was a good time to a make a will whereby he would leave all of his share in the property to his son. In 2009, Matthew moved out and rented a room in a house a few doors away because he was it very difficult to study for his final exams in the apartment due to elevated noise levels. This caused an argument between Alex and Paul which resulted in Paul removing all of Alex's possessions, including his plasma screen television and his X Box, from the living room and locking the living room door. On hearing of this, Alex's girlfriend suggested that he live with her in her house and so Alex then moved out. Matthew and Alex believe that should pay them cent. Alternatively, Matthew suggests that the apartment should be sold. Alex is uncertain as to whether the apartment should be sold because its market value has fallen a great deal over the past couple of years due to the recession. Paul says that he is not willing to pay them rent and that they are welcome to stay in the apartment. He objects to selling the apartment because he will not be able to find another home in the vicinity that is suitable for him given its close proximity to his place of work. He is also concerned as to whether his son would be adequately provided for if he were to die unexpectedly. Advise the parties.

questions.description: Answer (a) and (b) (a) Explain the ways in which a Will may be revoked under Irish succession law.
questions.text: Answer (a) and (b) (a) Explain the ways in which a Will may be revoked under Irish succession law. AND (b) Briefly discuss the rules and principles which dictate how an intestate's estate should be distributed.

questions.description: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The…
questions.text: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The influence of equity in Irish Land Law

questions.description: In 1980, Jenny inherited a small cottage from her uncle. Advise Jack.
questions.text: In 1980, Jenny inherited a small cottage from her uncle. She visited the property on a couple of occasions but decided that she did not wish to live there as it was much too quiet and isolated and her job friends were located in the city. Nonetheless, she decided to keep the house as somewhere to go for a break some weekends, holiday home in the summer and ultimately a retirement home. She asked the neighbour next door -- Charles - 'to keep an eye' on the cottage for her and gave him a spare key in the event that an emergency arose. However, work became very busy for Jenny that year, the result of which was that she never had the chance to stay in the cottage. A couple of years later, Charles began using the shed at the rear of the cottage to store his bicycle and his garden tools. In 1985 he decided to out some carry out some repairs on the shed as well as the grounds which had become quite dilapidated and in a state of disrepair. This work, which included a new gate and padlock (for which he had the only key) cost him €500. Since Jenny had not been in contact with Charles, he never told her about this. A year later, Charles stacked to use the house to accommodate friends who came to visit. He also staffed to rent it out during the summer months and kept the money for himself. In 2005, Charles sold his land to Jack end handed over the keys to Jenny's house. He informed Jack that Jenny had not been around for years. Jack continued to use Jenny's property as if it was his own. In early 2010, Jenny returned to her cottage and demanded her keys back. Advise Jack.

questions.description: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland,…
questions.text: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland, in particular, in the area of security of tenure and tenancy termination procedures. Consider whether the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 has succeeded in its aim of providing a clear legislative framework for tenancy obligations for landlords and tenants in Ireland.

questions.description: Ted successfully applied for a mortgage in 2008 so that he could build an… Mortgage.
questions.text: Ted successfully applied for a mortgage in 2008 so that he could build an extension onto his shop premises in a small village in Co. Galway. Over the past few years, Ted used up all of his savings to cover his mortgage repayments in the absence of any profit being generated from his business. Unfortunately over the past year, he has started to fall into arrears on his mortgage repayments. Advise the bank what its options are in terms of enforcing its security on the loan to Ted. Refer to legal and policy developments in your answer.

questions.description: In March 2011, Julia, who was an elderly lady, discovered that she had a… Advise Jeff and Dylan as to the terms and…
questions.text: In March 2011, Julia, who was an elderly lady, discovered that she had a lump in her leg. Upon further investigation at the hospital, it was decided that the lump was cancerous, should be removed as soon as possible and further treatment would be required. Before Julia went into hospital for treatment, she decided that she had better draw up a will making provision for her husband, Jeff. She didn't feel there was any need to include her son, Dylan, as she believed that when Jeff died, Dylan would ultimately inherit everything. Julia asked her solicitor, Mary, if she could draw up the will according to her instructions and drop it by her house for signature the week before the operation was due to take place. However, a few days later Julia won €50,000 on a scratch card which she collected. She decided that it would be important to include this in her will also. When Mary called to the house with the will, Julia signed the will in the presence of Mary and Dylan. Jeff was walking into the room next door when Julia was initialling the will. "I think you are supposed to watch me doing this love!" Julia called out to him. "I can see you signing it darling" he replied as he had his back turned. After Mary left the house, Julia suddenly remembered the money that she had won on the scratch card and added it to the bottom of the will, indicating that it was to go to Dylan. She thought that she should get her signature witnessed so asked both Dylan and his friend (who happened to be there at the time) to sign the will. Later that evening, Jeff came in and sat by the fire and keep it going. The will which was struck amidst the newspapers that were thrown into the fire was destroyed as a result. That night, Julia died in her sleep. Dylan claims that he is entitled to the €50,000 that his mother intended to leave to him but Jeff disagrees. Advise Jeff and Dylan as to the terms and validity of the will.

questions.description: Discuss the essential characteristics of an easement and explain the ways in which an easement may be acquired.
questions.text: Discuss the essential characteristics of an easement and explain the ways in which an easement may be acquired. Use relevant legal authority to support your answer.

questions.description: According to the Property Registration Authority of Ireland, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive…
questions.text: According to the Property Registration Authority of Ireland, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well developed system of land registration. Since the foundation of the Land Registry in 1892, there has been a gradual, ongoing and continuous programme of movement away from the older and limited system of recording deeds (in the Registry of Deeds), to the more modern, flexible and comprehensive 'title registration' system provided through the Land Registry. In light of the above statement, critically assess the development and operation of the system for recording property transactions in relation to property in Ireland.

questions.description: In 2002, Jack, a farmer, sold one of his fields to Champion Developments… Advise Champion Developments Ltd.
questions.text: In 2002, Jack, a farmer, sold one of his fields to Champion Developments Ltd. The field was adjacent to a dual carriageway which the local authorities had indicated would be upgraded to motorway status in the near future. Champion Developments Ltd bought the property from Jack with the intention of building a large motorway service station on the site. Once the sale of the land was complete, Champion Developments Ltd wrote a letter to Jack asking him if he could remove his cattle from the field. They also requested that he place one of their advertising signs on the gate nearest the road. However, Jack, being a fairly laid back type of person, left these tasks on the long finger. In fact, due to various delays in the motorway development, Jack continued to graze his cattle on the land for a number of years. In 2008, Jack left farming, sold his sheep and retired to Turkey. Before he left, he made an agreement to lease the field to his neighbour, Frank, so that he could grow some mushrooms. Frank erected a number of structures in the field to serve as growing rooms. In 2010, the Chief Executive of Champion Developments Ltd was driving by the field and noticed that there was some kind of unusual looking structure in it. He parked the car and looked in over the gate at the structure in the field. He decided to notify his solicitor who wrote to Frank requesting that he leave this private property immediately. Frank replied and said he had intended on leaving anyway as he was getting tired of the mushroom farming and in any case, more traffic on a motorway would ruin his crops. At the beginning of this year, Champion Developments Ltd has finally started on the motorway development but Frank has decided he wants to remain on the land to try and grow more organic vegetables. Advise Champion Developments Ltd.

questions.description: Write a critical note on any [two]{.underline} of the following aspects of Irish Property Law: (a) Rights and…
questions.text: Write a critical note on any [two]{.underline} of the following aspects of Irish Property Law: (a) Rights and responsibilities of cohabitants in relation to property; (b) The role of equity in Irish Property Law; (c) Rights and responsibilities of joint tenants in relation to property. Candidates should refer to legislation and case law, where relevant, in their answers.

questions.description: Matthew died last month from a severe heart attack, leaving a net estate…
questions.text: Matthew died last month from a severe heart attack, leaving a net estate worth €500,000. Matthew made a will in 2005. The only bequest in the will was one of "€50,000 to my best friend, Paul Simpson". However, in the will, the figure "€50,000" has been crossed out and the figure "€100,000" written above it in black pen (the rest of the will had been typewritten). Matthew's solicitor now admits that there was a mistake in the drafting of the will. The week after Matthew had first given instructions as to the terms of the will, the solicitor's secretary had received a phone call from Matthew saying that he felt he should leave more money to his dear friend Paul and asking the solicitor to change the amount of the bequest to Paul Simpson to €100,000. Unfortunately, the secretary, who was leaving to go on holidays that day, completely forgot to pass the message on to the solicitor. The solicitor is not sure how the figure of €100,000 came to appear on the will but believes that it is in the testator's handwriting. One of the witnesses to the will, Jenny, was cohabiting with Paul when the will was executed and the couple were married two months later. Matthew was a bachelor and had no children. His parents both died twenty years ago. However, he was survived by one of his sisters, Julie, with whom he had lost touch. His other twin sisters, Jackie and Ellie, died ten years ago. At the time of Matthew's death, Jackie was survived by her children, Jack and Jill, and Ellie was survived by her two grandchildren, Tom and Jerry. Advise as to the proper distribution of Matthew's estate.

questions.description: The Succession Act 1965 "...is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow…
questions.text: The Succession Act 1965 "...is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow (the legal right) and to make proper provision for his children in accordance with his means. It is not based on a duty to provide maintenance for his widow nor is it limited in its application to children who were dependent on him". Per Kenny J, FM v. TAM (1972) 106 ILTR 82, 86. Critically discuss this statement with reference to case law.

questions.description: One Friday evening, while out exploring with his dog on his father's land,… Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: One Friday evening, while out exploring with his dog on his father's land, 15 year old Julien discovered a hidden entrance to an old disused mine which was accessible from both his father's land and that of his neighbour, Sydney. Julien and his dog walked over a worn and muddy cardboard sign on the ground which read "Keep out - Danger". However, Julien and his dog carried on regardless and ventured further into the mine. After a few minutes, Julien stumbled upon a shiny object which appeared to be gold as well as some other pieces of old fashioned jewellery which were half buried in the ground. He wiped off the soil and saw that on the back of it there was an engraving "All my love to my dearest A, always and forever". Julien dropped the object and the other artefacts into the pocket of his jacket. Just then, Jake, who had sold Julien's father the land 12 years previously, appeared in the mine with Julien's father and claimed that he should have the find as it belonged to him. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the discovered objects.

questions.description: Under Landlord and Tenant Law, a landlord cannot unreasonably withhold his consent to a change of use or alienation.
questions.text: Under Landlord and Tenant Law, a landlord cannot unreasonably withhold his consent to a change of use or alienation. Discuss.

Mr. Chan has issued a new lease to Mr. Can of old cellars which lie below and adjacent to property retained by him. The lease agreement specifically states that the cellars will be used for a new Turkish restaurant. However, upon inspection of these cellars, the local Health Authority has advised that the ventilation system which is in place is wholly inadequate and a new ventilation system will have to be installed. Mr. Can has acted accordingly and hired a company to install the new system which involves fixing new piping to the property that has been retained by the landlord. Mr. Chan immediately questions Mr. Can's right to do this and now seeks your advice. Discuss with reference to relevant case law.
